subject: Project Management
title: ARPANET: The Internet's Birth
description: A 60-second lesson on ARPANET: The Internet's Birth.

hook.text: In 1969, a computer at UCLA sent a message to a computer at Stanford. It crashed after two letters.

buildup.text: The message was 'LOGIN.' The system crashed at 'LO.' The first internet message was 'Lo.'

discovery.text: ARPANET was funded by the U.S. military to create a network that could survive a nuclear attack.

twist.text: Scientists used it not for war but to share research — and to send the first email.

climax.text: A military network became the backbone of the internet, connecting 5 billion people today.

punchline.text: The internet's first message was a typo. It changed everything.

quiz.question: What was the first message sent over ARPANET?
